findings.message: New runtime dependency 'requests' is added without any version constraint. Unpinned/unbounded dependencies make builds non-reproducible and expose the project to silently-shipping a future breaking or compromised release. All sibling deps in this manifest use lower bounds (e.g. 'tenacity>=9.0', 'pybreaker>=1.2').
findings.suggestion: Pin a lower bound consistent with the rest of the manifest, e.g. 'requests>=2.32'.

findings.message: 'requests' duplicates capability already covered by the existing toolchain. The project ships FastAPI/Starlette and uses 'httpx' (declared in dev deps for the test client); httpx is the modern, async-capable HTTP client that pairs naturally with the async FastAPI/OpenTelemetry stack here. Adding 'requests' introduces a second HTTP client surface, doubles the supply-chain footprint, and 'requests' is in maintenance-only mode upstream.
findings.suggestion: Drop 'requests' and use 'httpx' (promote it from dev to runtime deps if needed); it works in both sync and async contexts and is already instrumented well by OpenTelemetry.

findings.message: Justification for the new dependency is not visible in the diff. Adding a runtime HTTP client is a notable surface-area / supply-chain change; the change should be accompanied by a clear use-case (which call site needs it) so reviewers can confirm an existing dep (httpx, urllib) doesn't already cover it.
findings.suggestion: In the PR description, cite the concrete call site that requires 'requests' and why httpx/urllib aren't sufficient.
